items.q: What are the three elements of Beck's negative triad?
items.correct: Negative views of self, world, and future.
items.misconception: Negative views of past, present, and future.
items.why: Students often substitute 'past' for 'self' or 'world'. The triad is about the DOMAINS of negative thought (self/world/future), not time periods.

items.q: What is the behaviourist view on the nature-nurture debate?
items.correct: Strongly nurture - we are shaped by environmental conditioning.
items.why: Behaviourists reject innate ideas (tabula rasa - blank slate). Students soften this to 'a mix'. Watson and Skinner explicitly attributed nearly all behaviour to environment.
Students reverse IV and DV. The IV is what you CHANGE (input); the DV is what you MEASURE (outcome). Mnemonic: 'I change the I.'

items.q: Top-down offender profiling was developed by:
items.why: It's FBI. “Canter” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Differential association theory was proposed by:
items.why: It's Sutherland. “Eysenck” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year did Lombroso publish his atavistic form theory?
items.why: The real figure is 1876 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
